title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox chief Craig Breslow's trade deadline plans remain fluid as the team reassesses options ahead of the deadline.
Boston's playoff odds have increased to 44.3% after a recent win streak, impacting trade decisions.
The team may focus on acquiring a right-handed hitter, as they evaluate their current roster during this period.

title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket is for sale for $12.5 million as owners Evan and Maria Marley retire.
The sale includes the restaurant, a 46-seat dining area, staff housing, and a 20-spot parking lot.
The Marleys, who opened the pizzeria in 2001, will assist with transition upon finding a buyer.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: The France national soccer team left Boston after over a month at the Four Seasons Hotel.
France heads to Miami to face England for third place, following a 2-0 semi-final loss to Spain.
While in Boston, the team practiced at Bentley University and rented the entire Four Seasons Hotel.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon announced his production company is developing a movie about Karen Read's controversial murder case.
Artists Equity's adaptation joins multiple production efforts, including works by Lifetime, Prime Video, and Netflix.
Karen Read was acquitted of charges in 2022 but faces ongoing legal battles, including a wrongful death lawsuit.

title: Steve Carell and his wife, Nancy, celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Steve and Nancy Carell celebrated Nancy's upcoming 60th birthday at Tresca, a North End restaurant in Boston.
Richard Ansara, Tresca's co-owner, shared a photo with Steve Carell on the restaurant's Instagram page.
Nancy Carell, born in Cohasset, has appeared in films with Steve and was a cast member on 'SNL'.

title: Some Saugus residents stunned by high water bills, with one nearing $200,000
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Saugus residents report abnormally high water bills, with amounts up to $192,000 being contested.
The billing issue arose after the town began replacing water meters and upgrading the monitoring system.
Unclear causes of the problem have led to payment challenges, penalties, and property liens for affected homeowners.

title: Review & setlist: Bob Dylan was spookily stellar on a smoky night in Boston
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bob Dylan performed at Leader Bank Pavilion in Boston, focusing on newer material over his classic hits.
Dylan's set included 16 songs, with standout performances from his 'Rough and Rowdy Ways' album.
The concert featured opening acts Brittney Spencer and Jimmie Vaughan, with Dylan closing on 'I Shall Be Released'.

title: The middle seat will become a table on United’s newest Airbus jets
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines introduces middle seats turned into tables on new Airbus A321XLR aircraft in Economy Plus.
The new design features a shared table with leather-like covering and cup areas, providing extra space for passengers.
United's A321XLR will be available for domestic flights this fall and international service by early 2027.

title: Original ‘Gilmore Girls’ house is on the market, and it’s not in Stars Hollow
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The 'Gilmore Girls' pilot house in Markham, Ontario, is listed for $2.76 million USD.
The home, originally built in 1875, features four bedrooms, four bathrooms, and a historic barn.
The listing has attracted attention from fans for its role in the 'Gilmore Girls' series pilot.

title: Longtime Maine soccer coach recognized by FIFA as ‘Unsung Hero’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Gary Newell, a Maine soccer coach, was honored as FIFA's 'Unsung Hero' at the 2026 World Cup in Boston.
Newell, a volunteer, was recognized for decades of contributing to youth soccer, receiving a miniature World Cup trophy.
Newell was surprised with a video message from England's Jude Bellingham, acknowledging his dedication to youth soccer.
